text: What is the depth of the Mariana Trench in meters?
explanation: The Mariana Trench reaches 10,994 meters at Challenger Deep, the deepest known point on Earth.

text: What is the area of Antarctica in square kilometers?
explanation: Antarctica covers approximately 14.2 million square kilometers, making it the fifth-largest continent.

text: What is the length of the Amazon River in kilometers?
explanation: The Amazon River stretches approximately 6,992 km, making it the world's longest river by some measurements.

text: At what elevation in meters is Mount Everest's summit?
explanation: Mount Everest was remeasured in 2020 by China and Nepal and confirmed at 8,848.86 meters above sea level.

text: What is the population of Tokyo metropolitan area in millions as of 2023?
explanation: The Greater Tokyo Area is the world's most populous metropolitan area with about 37.4 million residents.

text: How many countries share a land border with Russia?
explanation: Russia borders 14 countries, the most of any nation, spanning from Norway in the northwest to North Korea in the southeast.

text: What is the distance from Earth to the Moon in kilometers?
explanation: The Moon orbits Earth at an average distance of 384,400 km, though this varies between about 362,600 and 405,400 km.

text: What is the area of the Sahara Desert in square kilometers?
explanation: The Sahara Desert spans approximately 9.2 million square kilometers, roughly the size of the United States.

text: How many islands make up the Philippines?
explanation: The Philippines is an archipelago officially comprising 7,641 islands, though only about 2,000 are inhabited.

text: What is the length of the Great Barrier Reef in kilometers?
explanation: The Great Barrier Reef stretches over 2,300 km along the northeast coast of Australia, visible from space.

text: What is the elevation of Lake Titicaca above sea level in meters?
explanation: Lake Titicaca sits at 3,812 meters above sea level on the border of Bolivia and Peru, making it the world's highest large lake.

text: How many time zones does Russia span?
explanation: Russia spans 11 time zones, more than any other country, stretching from UTC+2 to UTC+12.

text: What is the width of the English Channel at its narrowest point in kilometers?
explanation: The Strait of Dover, the English Channel's narrowest point, is just 33.3 km wide between Dover and Calais.

text: What is the flow rate of Niagara Falls in cubic meters per second?
explanation: Niagara Falls discharges an average of 2,400 cubic meters per second, making it one of the most powerful waterfalls by volume.

text: How many meters deep is Lake Baikal at its deepest point?
explanation: Lake Baikal in Siberia is the world's deepest lake at 1,642 meters and holds about 20% of the world's unfrozen surface fresh water.

text: What percentage of Earth's surface is covered by oceans?
explanation: Oceans cover approximately 71% of Earth's surface, with the Pacific alone covering more area than all land combined.

text: What is the population of India in millions as of 2024?
explanation: India surpassed China in 2023 to become the world's most populous country with approximately 1.44 billion people.

text: What is the length of the Trans-Siberian Railway in kilometers?
explanation: The Trans-Siberian Railway stretches 9,289 km from Moscow to Vladivostok, the longest railway line in the world.

text: How many meters tall is Kilimanjaro, Africa's highest peak?
explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania rises 5,895 meters above sea level, making it the highest peak in Africa and the tallest free-standing mountain.

text: What is the area of the Mediterranean Sea in square kilometers?
explanation: The Mediterranean Sea covers approximately 2.5 million square kilometers and is bordered by 21 countries across three continents.

text: What is the speed of light in kilometers per second?
explanation: Light travels at exactly 299,792.458 km/s in a vacuum, a fundamental physical constant denoted as c.

text: How many confirmed moons does Jupiter have as of 2024?
explanation: As of 2024 Jupiter has 95 confirmed moons, the most of any planet, including the four large Galilean moons discovered in 1610.

text: What is the half-life of Carbon-14 in years?
explanation: Carbon-14 has a half-life of 5,730 years, which is why radiocarbon dating is effective for materials up to about 50,000 years old.

text: What is the diameter of Earth in kilometers?
explanation: Earth's mean diameter is 12,742 km; it is slightly flattened at the poles with an equatorial diameter of 12,756 km.

text: How many elements are in the periodic table as of 2024?
explanation: The periodic table currently contains 118 confirmed elements, with element 118 (oganesson) being the most recently named in 2016.

text: What is the average distance from Earth to the Sun in millions of kilometers?
explanation: One astronomical unit (AU), the average Earth-Sun distance, equals 149.6 million kilometers or about 8.3 light-minutes.

text: How many base pairs are in the human genome?
explanation: The human genome contains approximately 3.2 billion base pairs of DNA encoding roughly 20,000 protein-coding genes.

text: At what temperature in Celsius does iron melt?
explanation: Iron melts at 1,538°C (2,800°F), a high melting point that made smelting it one of the great technological breakthroughs of human history.

text: How many neurons are in the human brain?
explanation: The human brain contains approximately 86 billion neurons, each capable of forming thousands of synaptic connections.

text: What is the wavelength of green light in nanometers?
explanation: Green light occupies the middle of the visible spectrum at around 550 nanometers, the wavelength to which human eyes are most sensitive.

text: How many liters of blood does the human heart pump per minute at rest?
explanation: At rest, the human heart pumps about 5 liters of blood per minute; during intense exercise this can rise to 25 liters per minute.

text: What is the boiling point of liquid nitrogen in Celsius?
explanation: Nitrogen boils at −196°C (−321°F) at standard atmospheric pressure, making liquid nitrogen an extremely effective coolant.

text: How many times does the average human heart beat per day?
explanation: At an average resting rate of about 70 beats per minute, the human heart beats roughly 100,000 times a day.

text: What is the surface temperature of the Sun in Kelvin?
explanation: The photosphere, the Sun's visible surface, has an effective temperature of about 5,778 K (5,505°C).

text: How many calories are in one gram of fat?
explanation: Fat provides 9 kilocalories per gram, more than twice the energy density of carbohydrates or protein (both about 4 kcal/g).

text: What is the atomic number of gold?
explanation: Gold (Au) has atomic number 79, meaning each gold atom contains 79 protons; its high density and stability make it ideal for jewelry and electronics.

text: How many years does it take Neptune to orbit the Sun?
explanation: Neptune takes 164.8 Earth years to complete one orbit of the Sun; it has completed only one orbit since its discovery in 1846.

text: What is the speed of sound in air at 20°C in meters per second?
explanation: Sound travels at approximately 343 m/s (1,235 km/h) in dry air at 20°C, roughly one kilometre every three seconds.

text: What is the GDP of the United States in trillion USD as of 2024?
explanation: The US GDP reached approximately $27.4 trillion in 2024, maintaining its position as the world's largest national economy.

text: What percentage of global GDP does China represent as of 2023?
explanation: China's economy represents approximately 17% of world GDP by market exchange rates, and about 19% by purchasing power parity.

text: What was the global inflation rate in percent in 2022?
explanation: Global consumer price inflation hit 8.8% in 2022, driven by post-pandemic supply disruptions and the energy price shock from the war in Ukraine.

text: How many billionaires were there in the world as of 2024?
explanation: Forbes counted 2,781 billionaires worldwide in 2024 with a combined net worth of $14.2 trillion.

text: What is the GDP of Japan in trillion USD as of 2023?
explanation: Japan's GDP stood at approximately $4.2 trillion in 2023, making it the world's fourth-largest economy after the US, China, and Germany.

text: What is the federal minimum wage in the United States in dollars per hour as of 2024?
explanation: The US federal minimum wage has remained at $7.25 per hour since 2009, though many states and cities have set higher local minimums.

text: What was Apple's market capitalization in trillion USD at peak in 2024?
explanation: Apple's market capitalization peaked above $3.5 trillion in 2024, making it the first company to reach that milestone.

text: What percentage of global trade goes through container ships?
explanation: Approximately 80% of global trade by volume is transported by sea, with container ships carrying the majority of manufactured goods.

text: What was the unemployment rate in the United States in percent in 2023?
explanation: The US unemployment rate averaged about 3.6% in 2023, remaining near historically low levels despite high interest rates.

text: What is the total value of the global real estate market in trillion USD as of 2023?
explanation: Global real estate is valued at roughly $326 trillion, representing the world's largest asset class and about 60% of mainstream assets.

text: What percentage of US workers are employed in the service sector as of 2023?
explanation: About 80% of US workers are employed in the service sector, reflecting the shift from manufacturing to knowledge and service-based industries.

text: What was the total US national debt in trillion dollars as of 2024?
explanation: The US national debt crossed $34 trillion in January 2024, having grown substantially since the COVID-19 pandemic spending.

text: How many barrels of oil does Saudi Arabia produce per day on average in 2023?
explanation: Saudi Arabia produced approximately 9 million barrels of oil per day in 2023 after OPEC+ production cuts, remaining the world's top exporter.

text: What was the global e-commerce market size in trillion USD in 2023?
explanation: Global e-commerce sales reached approximately $5.8 trillion in 2023, driven by rising smartphone adoption and improved logistics.

text: What is the average salary of a software engineer in the United States in USD per year as of 2024?
explanation: The median annual salary for software engineers in the US is approximately $120,000, with senior roles at top tech firms often exceeding $200,000.

text: What was the price of Bitcoin in USD at its all-time high in 2024?
explanation: Bitcoin set a new all-time high of approximately $73,750 on March 14, 2024, surpassing its previous peak set in November 2021.

text: What percentage of world electricity was generated from renewables in 2023?
explanation: Renewables generated about 30% of global electricity in 2023, with solar being the fastest-growing source for the 19th consecutive year.

text: In what year was the Roman Colosseum completed?
explanation: The Colosseum in Rome was completed in 80 CE under Emperor Titus, and its inaugural games lasted 100 days.

text: How many years did the Hundred Years' War last?
explanation: Despite its name, the Hundred Years' War between England and France lasted 116 years, from 1337 to 1453.

text: In what year was the Magna Carta signed?
explanation: King John of England sealed the Magna Carta at Runnymede in 1215, establishing for the first time that the king was subject to the rule of law.

text: In what year did construction of the Great Wall of China begin?
explanation: Early walls that eventually became the Great Wall were built starting around 700 BCE; the most familiar Ming Dynasty sections date to 1368–1644 CE.

text: How many people died in the Black Death pandemic of the 14th century?
explanation: The Black Death killed an estimated 30–50 million people across Eurasia from 1347–1351, wiping out roughly one-third of Europe's population.

text: In what year did the Berlin Wall fall?
explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, after 28 years of dividing East and West Berlin, marking a pivotal moment in ending the Cold War.

text: How many years did the Roman Empire last at its maximum count?
explanation: The Western Roman Empire lasted from 27 BCE to 476 CE — a span of 503 years — though the Eastern Byzantine Empire survived until 1453 CE.

text: In what year was the first transatlantic telegraph cable completed?
explanation: The first permanent transatlantic telegraph cable was completed in 1866 after earlier attempts in 1857 and 1858 failed, enabling near-instant communication between Europe and North America.

text: How many soldiers died in World War I?
explanation: World War I caused approximately 20 million military deaths, with millions more civilian fatalities from violence, famine, and the 1918 flu pandemic.

text: In what year did the United States declare independence?
explanation: The Second Continental Congress adopted the Declaration of Independence on July 4, 1776, marking the birth of the United States.

text: How many years did the Byzantine Empire last?
explanation: The Byzantine Empire endured from 395 CE to 1453 CE — over 1,058 years — outlasting the Western Roman Empire by nearly a millennium.

text: In what year was the printing press invented by Johannes Gutenberg?
explanation: Johannes Gutenberg developed his movable-type printing press around 1440 in Mainz, Germany, triggering a revolution in information dissemination.

text: How many voyages did Christopher Columbus make to the Americas?
explanation: Columbus made four voyages to the Americas between 1492 and 1504, exploring the Caribbean, Central America, and South America.

text: In what year did the French Revolution begin?
explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille on July 14, fundamentally transforming France and influencing democracies worldwide.

text: How many years after the Wright Brothers' first flight did humans land on the Moon?
explanation: The Wright Brothers flew in 1903 and Apollo 11 landed on the Moon in 1969 — an astonishing 66-year span of aviation progress.

text: In what year was the Great Fire of London?
explanation: The Great Fire of London burned for four days in September 1666, destroying 13,200 houses and 87 churches including the original St Paul's Cathedral.

text: How many episodes of The Simpsons have aired as of 2025?
explanation: The Simpsons has aired approximately 768 episodes through its first 36 seasons, making it the longest-running American animated series.

text: How many films are in the Marvel Cinematic Universe as of 2024?
explanation: The MCU comprises 33 theatrical films through 2024, beginning with Iron Man (2008) and forming the highest-grossing film franchise in history.

text: How many copies of the Harry Potter series have been sold worldwide?
explanation: The Harry Potter series has sold over 600 million copies worldwide, translated into 85 languages, making it the best-selling book series in history.

text: What is the total box office gross of Avatar in billion USD?
explanation: Avatar (2009) has grossed approximately $2.92 billion worldwide, holding the record as the highest-grossing film of all time.

text: How many seasons of Game of Thrones were produced?
explanation: Game of Thrones ran for 8 seasons from 2011 to 2019 on HBO, totalling 73 episodes and becoming one of the most-watched TV dramas ever.

text: How many Grammy Awards has Beyoncé won as of 2024?
explanation: Beyoncé holds the record for most Grammy Awards won with 32, surpassing conductor Georg Solti's previous record of 31.

text: How many weeks did 'Old Town Road' spend at number 1 on the Billboard Hot 100?
explanation: 'Old Town Road' by Lil Nas X spent 19 weeks at number 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2019, the longest-running number 1 in chart history.

text: How many copies has Minecraft sold worldwide as of 2023?
explanation: Minecraft has sold over 238 million copies across all platforms, making it the best-selling video game of all time.

text: What is the running time of the Lord of the Rings extended edition trilogy in minutes?
explanation: The Lord of the Rings extended edition trilogy runs for approximately 686 minutes (11 hours 26 minutes) across the three films.

text: How many subscribers does YouTube's most subscribed channel, MrBeast, have in millions as of 2024?
explanation: MrBeast became YouTube's most subscribed individual creator, surpassing 310 million subscribers by late 2024.

text: How many albums has Taylor Swift released as of 2024?
explanation: Taylor Swift released her 11th studio album, 'The Tortured Poets Department,' in April 2024, continuing her dominance of the music industry.

text: How many episodes does the original Star Trek TV series have?
explanation: Star Trek: The Original Series ran for three seasons from 1966 to 1969, comprising 79 episodes that launched one of the most enduring science fiction franchises.

text: How many countries competed in the 2024 Paris Summer Olympics?
explanation: 204 National Olympic Committees sent athletes to the 2024 Paris Olympics, which featured 329 events across 32 sports.

text: How many total goals were scored in the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: The 2022 Qatar World Cup saw 172 goals scored across 64 matches, averaging 2.69 goals per game in a tournament widely praised for drama.

text: How many active satellites are in orbit around Earth as of 2024?
explanation: There are approximately 9,000 active satellites in Earth orbit as of 2024, with SpaceX's Starlink constellation making up more than half of them.

text: How many people died from COVID-19 worldwide by the end of 2023?
explanation: The WHO recorded over 7 million confirmed COVID-19 deaths globally by end of 2023, though excess mortality estimates suggest the true toll was much higher.

text: What percentage of the world's population uses the internet as of 2024?
explanation: About 67% of the world's population — some 5.4 billion people — was online as of 2024, with Africa showing the fastest growth in connectivity.

text: How many terawatt-hours of electricity did the world consume in 2023?
explanation: Global electricity consumption reached approximately 29,165 TWh in 2023, continuing a long-term growth trend driven by emerging economies and electrification.

text: What was the global average surface temperature increase above pre-industrial levels in Celsius in 2023?
explanation: 2023 was the hottest year on record at 1.45°C above pre-industrial levels, edging toward the 1.5°C Paris Agreement threshold.

text: How many Starlink satellites has SpaceX launched as of 2024?
explanation: SpaceX surpassed 6,000 Starlink satellites in orbit by 2024, creating the world's largest satellite constellation for broadband internet.

text: How many electric vehicles were sold worldwide in 2023?
explanation: About 14 million electric vehicles were sold globally in 2023, representing roughly 18% of all new car sales, with China accounting for 60% of global EV sales.

text: What was the global smartphone shipment count in millions in 2023?
explanation: Global smartphone shipments totalled approximately 1.17 billion units in 2023, down from a peak of 1.54 billion in 2016 due to longer replacement cycles.

text: How many countries have signed the Paris Agreement on climate change as of 2024?
explanation: 195 parties (194 countries + the EU) have signed the Paris Agreement, making it one of the most widely ratified international environmental treaties.

text: What was the global refugee count in millions as of end of 2023?
explanation: UNHCR reported 43.4 million refugees worldwide at end of 2023, the highest figure since World War II, driven by conflicts in Sudan, Ukraine, and Afghanistan.

text: How many gigawatts of solar power capacity were installed globally by end of 2023?
explanation: Cumulative global solar PV capacity exceeded 1,600 GW by end of 2023 after a record-breaking year of installations driven by falling panel costs.

text: How many people were estimated to be food insecure worldwide in 2023?
explanation: The UN reported approximately 733 million people faced hunger globally in 2023, a number that has risen since the pandemic reversed decades of progress.

text: What was the US federal funds rate target upper bound in percent at end of 2023?
explanation: The Federal Reserve held its target federal funds rate at 5.25–5.50% at end of 2023, a 22-year high reached after 11 rate hikes to combat inflation.

text: How many active nuclear reactors are there worldwide as of 2024?
explanation: There are approximately 440 operational nuclear reactors worldwide as of 2024, generating about 10% of global electricity in 32 countries.

text: How many bones does a human adult have?
explanation: Adult humans have 206 bones; babies are born with about 270, but many fuse together during childhood and early adulthood.

text: How many stars are estimated to be in the Milky Way galaxy in billions?
explanation: Current estimates place the number of stars in the Milky Way at 100–400 billion; 250 billion is a widely cited midpoint estimate.

text: What was the global military spending in trillion USD in 2023?
explanation: Global military spending hit a record $2.2 trillion in 2023, according to the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute.

text: What is the annual revenue of Walmart in billion USD as of fiscal year 2024?
explanation: Walmart's annual revenue exceeded $648 billion in fiscal year 2024, making it the world's largest company by revenue for over a decade.

text: What was the global advertising market size in billion USD in 2023?
explanation: The global advertising market was valued at approximately $889 billion in 2023, with digital advertising surpassing two-thirds of total spend.

text: In what year was the Eiffel Tower completed?
explanation: The Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889 as the entrance arch for the 1889 World's Fair in Paris, and stood as the world's tallest structure for 41 years.

text: How many years did the Cold War last from 1947 to its end?
explanation: The Cold War lasted approximately 44 years, from 1947 when the Truman Doctrine was announced to 1991 when the Soviet Union dissolved.

text: In what year did the Titanic sink?
explanation: RMS Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, after striking an iceberg, killing more than 1,500 people in one of the deadliest peacetime maritime disasters.

text: How many slaves were transported to the Americas during the transatlantic slave trade in millions?
explanation: Approximately 12.5 million enslaved Africans were forcibly transported to the Americas over the course of the transatlantic slave trade from the 16th to 19th centuries.

text: How many books has Stephen King published as of 2024?
explanation: Stephen King has published approximately 65 novels and story collections since his debut Carrie in 1974, making him one of the most prolific novelists ever.

text: What is the total worldwide gross of the Fast and Furious franchise in billion USD?
explanation: The Fast & Furious franchise has grossed over $7.3 billion worldwide, making it one of the top five highest-grossing film franchises of all time.

text: How many seasons did Friends run for?
explanation: Friends ran for 10 seasons from 1994 to 2004 on NBC, comprising 236 episodes and remaining one of the most-watched sitcoms in television history.

text: How many songs did Michael Jackson release as studio albums?
explanation: Michael Jackson released 10 studio albums in his lifetime, from Got to Be There (1972) through Invincible (2001).

text: How many views does 'Baby Shark Dance' have on YouTube in billions as of 2024?
explanation: Baby Shark Dance has over 14 billion views on YouTube, making it the most-viewed video in the platform's history since 2020.

text: How many characters are in the standard Unicode emoji set as of 2023?
explanation: Unicode 15.1 (2023) brought the total number of standardized emoji to 3,664, covering faces, objects, flags, and symbols.

text: How many metric tons of plastic waste were produced globally in 2022?
explanation: Global plastic waste production reached approximately 400 million metric tons in 2022, with less than 10% being recycled.

text: How many monthly active users does TikTok have in millions as of 2024?
explanation: TikTok surpassed 1.6 billion monthly active users by 2024, becoming one of the world's most popular social media platforms just seven years after launch.

text: What was the global average life expectancy in years as of 2023?
explanation: Global average life expectancy was 73.3 years as of 2023, recovering after dipping during the COVID-19 pandemic but still below the pre-pandemic trajectory.

text: How many wildfires burned in Canada during the record 2023 fire season in thousands?
explanation: Canada's 2023 wildfire season was the worst in recorded history, with approximately 6,600 fires burning over 18 million hectares.

text: How many people use generative AI tools at least weekly as of 2024 in millions?
explanation: Approximately 400 million people were using generative AI tools weekly by early 2024, driven by the rapid adoption of ChatGPT and its competitors.

text: What was India's GDP growth rate in percent in fiscal year 2023-24?
explanation: India's GDP grew at 8.2% in fiscal year 2023-24, making it the fastest-growing major economy in the world for the second consecutive year.
